topic: Raymond making a gift for their anniversary

turns.content: so Raymond thinks he is being sneaky. our anniversary is next week and he has been in the workshop every night this week with the door shut. he does not shut that door unless he is making something he does not want me to see
Ha, that's sweet -- a silversmith husband who still tries to surprise you with handmade gifts. Do you have any guesses about what he's working on, or are you resisting the urge to peek?

I am not peeking. I have some dignity. but I did hear the torch last night and then the polishing wheel this morning before sunrise. whatever it is, it involves turquoise because I saw him sorting stones on Tuesday. he tried to hide them under a rag when I walked in. subtle as a truck, that man

Sorting turquoise stones and firing up the torch before sunrise -- he's putting real time and intention into this. The fact that he still makes gifts by hand after all these years says a lot. Do you have a preference for what you'd like it to be, or are you genuinely happy with whatever he creates?

I do not care what it is. that is the truth. he could hand me a silver thimble and I would wear it around my neck. it is the making of it, you know. he puts hours into something and every one of those hours is him thinking about me. that is the gift
That's a beautiful way to see it -- the hours at the bench are the real gift, and the piece is just the physical form of that time and thought. Are you making or getting anything for him in return?

I am making him a new hatband. wool. been weaving it when he is in the workshop so we are both hiding things from each other like teenagers. his old hatband is falling apart and he refuses to replace it because his mother made it. so I am making one in the same colors
That's so thoughtful -- matching the colors of the one his mother made so it feels like a continuation, not a replacement. He's going to love that. How far along are you on it?

almost done. just need to finish the edges and add the ties. I have been working on it during lunch at the clinic. one of the nurses caught me weaving in my office and asked what I was doing. I told her it was a medical device. she did not question it

A medical device -- that's perfect. I'm sure she was confused but wisely decided not to press further. It sounds like you two have a good system going: both secretly making things for each other and both probably aware of exactly what's happening but playing along anyway.

that is marriage, you know. you pretend not to know things to let the other person have their moment. he knows I am making something. I know he is making something. we both act surprised next week and it is real somehow. the surprise is not what it is. the surprise is that after all this time we still bother

"The surprise is that after all this time we still bother" -- that's one of the most honest and lovely things I've heard about a long marriage. The effort is the point. I hope you both get to enjoy your surprised faces next week. Happy anniversary to you and Raymond.
ahéhee'. I will let you know how the surprised faces turn out. now I need to go hide this hatband before he comes inside for coffee
Go hide it quick -- and enjoy the last few days of the mutual sneaking around. Happy anniversary when it comes. You two sound like you've got something really good.
